title: Ratha Yatra (Jagannath Rath Yatra)
intro: Ratha Yatra, also known as Jagannath Rath Yatra, is one of the most sacred and famous Hindu festivals celebrated mainly in Puri, Odisha.
description:
Ratha Yatra, also known as Jagannath Rath Yatra, is one of the most sacred and famous Hindu festivals celebrated mainly in Puri, Odisha. The festival is dedicated to Lord Jagannath (a form of Lord Vishnu), Lord Balabhadra, and Goddess Subhadra. Ratha Yatra is observed on the Dwitiya Tithi of Shukla Paksha in the Hindu month of Ashadha, usually during June or July.

According to Hindu traditions, on this auspicious day, Lord Jagannath, along with his brother Lord Balabhadra and sister Goddess Subhadra, leaves the temple and travels on grand chariots to give divine blessings and darshan to devotees. The journey symbolizes the Lord’s love for his devotees and his presence among the people.

The famous Puri Jagannath Rath Yatra is one of the largest religious festivals in the world, attracting millions of devotees. Three beautifully decorated chariots — Nandighosa (Lord Jagannath), Taladhwaja (Lord Balabhadra), and Darpadalana (Goddess Subhadra) — are pulled by devotees with great devotion. The festival represents faith, equality, devotion, and complete surrender to God.

Festival Month

Ashadha (June – July)

Significance / Importance

Ratha Yatra holds great religious and spiritual importance in Hinduism. It represents the divine journey of Lord Jagannath and his connection with devotees. The festival is considered an opportunity to receive the blessings of Lord Jagannath and experience divine grace.

Devotees believe that witnessing the Rath Yatra and participating in pulling the chariots brings spiritual benefits and blessings. The festival teaches equality, unity, devotion, love, and the importance of surrendering to God.

Puja Vidhi

1. Wake up early, take a holy bath, and wear clean clothes.
2. Worship Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra, and Goddess Subhadra.
3. Offer flowers, fruits, incense, lamps, and Naivedya.
4. Chant Jagannath Mantra and Vishnu Mantras.
5. Visit the Rath Yatra or watch the sacred procession with devotion.
6. Participate in the chariot pulling ceremony if possible.
7. Receive Prasad and pray for happiness, peace, and prosperity.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Jagannath Rath Yatra Katha
* Lord Jagannath Katha
* Jagannath Mantra
* Vishnu Mantra
* Jagannath Aarti
* Vishnu Aarti
* Vishnu Chalisa

faq.a1: Ratha Yatra is celebrated on the Dwitiya Tithi of Shukla Paksha in the Hindu month of Ashadha.
faq.a2: Ratha Yatra celebrates the journey of Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra, and Goddess Subhadra as they come out of the temple to bless devotees.
faq.q3: Where is the most famous Ratha Yatra held?
faq.a3: The most famous Ratha Yatra is held at the Jagannath Temple in Puri, Odisha.
faq.q4: Which deities participate in the Rath Yatra?
faq.a4: Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra, and Goddess Subhadra participate in the Rath Yatra.
faq.a5: Lord Jagannath is considered a form of Lord Vishnu and Lord Krishna.
faq.q6: How many chariots are used in Ratha Yatra?
faq.a6: Three main chariots are used — Nandighosa, Taladhwaja, and Darpadalana.
faq.q7: In which month is Ratha Yatra celebrated?
faq.a7: Ratha Yatra is usually celebrated in June or July.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Ratha Yatra?
faq.a8: Ratha Yatra symbolizes devotion, equality, love, unity, and the divine connection between God and devotees.
